{"key_event":"Brexit referendum amid austerity and surveillance expansion","reasoning":"Civil society remained extremely powerful, with referendums, media scrutiny, digital mobilization, and scandals forcing accountability, though austerity, legal-aid cuts, insecurity, polarization, and surveillance slightly checked social autonomy. State power stayed very high, but austerity-driven service retrenchment, devolution pressures, Brexit disruption, and legitimacy failures modestly outweighed gains in surveillance and central fiscal control.","society_change":-0.1,"society_power":9.4,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":9.1}
